Moderation Analysis: Schooling × Fossil-fuel share of Electricity

Yunting Du

2026-01-06

  • 1 Packages
  • 2 Paths
  • 3 Data cleaning
    • 3.1 Fossilfuel share data
    • 3.2 CO2 per capita data
    • 3.3 Mean years of schooling data
    • 3.4 GDP per capita data

1 Packages

library(dplyr)
library(readr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(car)
library(sandwich)
library(lmtest)
library(stargazer)
library(DescTools)
library(interactions)
library(knitr)
library(here)

2 Paths

path_energy <- here("owid-energy-data.csv")
path_co2    <- here("owid-co2-data.csv")
path_school <- here("average_years_of_schooling.csv")
path_gdp    <- here("GDP_per_capita.csv")

target_year <- 2023
energy <- read_csv(path_energy)
co2    <- read_csv(path_co2)
school <- read_csv(path_school)
gdp    <- read_csv(path_gdp)

3 Data cleaning

3.1 Fossilfuel share data

fossil_share_elec <- read_csv(path_energy, show_col_types = FALSE)

fossil_share_elec_2023 <- fossil_share_elec %>%
filter(nchar(iso_code) == 3, year == target_year) %>%
select(country, iso_code, year, fossil_share_elec)

head(fossil_share_elec_2023)
write_csv(
fossil_share_elec_2023,
here("output", paste0("owid-fossil-data-cleaned-", target_year, ".csv"))
)

3.2 CO2 per capita data

co2pc <- read_csv(path_co2, show_col_types = FALSE)

co2_per_capita_2023 <- co2pc %>%
filter(nchar(iso_code) == 3, year == target_year) %>%
select(country, iso_code, year, co2_per_capita)

head(co2_per_capita_2023)
write_csv(
co2_per_capita_2023,
here("output", paste0("owid-co2pc-data-cleaned-", target_year, ".csv"))
)

3.3 Mean years of schooling data

schooling_2023 <- read_csv(path_school, show_col_types = FALSE) %>%
transmute(
country   = Entity,
iso_code  = iso_code,
year      = year,
schooling = as.numeric(schooling)
) %>%
filter(nchar(iso_code) == 3, year == target_year)

head(schooling_2023)
write_csv(
schooling_2023,
here("output", paste0("schooling-cleaned-", target_year, ".csv"))
)

3.4 GDP per capita data

lines <- readLines(path_gdp, n = 300, warn = FALSE)
header_line <- which(grepl("Country Name", lines, fixed = TRUE))[1]

gdp_raw <- read_csv(path_gdp, skip = header_line - 1, show_col_types = FALSE)

year_idx <- match(as.character(target_year), names(gdp_raw))
if (is.na(year_idx)) stop("Year column not found. Please check if this file contains the target year.")

gdp_country_2023 <- tibble(
country = gdp_raw[[1]],
iso_code = gdp_raw[[2]],
year = target_year,
gdp_per_capita = as.numeric(gdp_raw[[year_idx]])
) %>%
filter(nchar(iso_code) == 3)

head(gdp_country_2023)
write_csv(
gdp_country_2023,
here("output", paste0("gdp-per-capita-cleaned-", target_year, ".csv"))
)

4 Data merging

merged_yr <- fossil_share_elec_2023 %>%
left_join(
co2_per_capita_2023 %>% select(iso_code, year, co2_per_capita),
by = c("iso_code", "year")
) %>%
left_join(
schooling_2023 %>% select(iso_code, year, schooling),
by = c("iso_code", "year")
) %>%
left_join(
gdp_country_2023 %>% select(iso_code, year, gdp_per_capita),
by = c("iso_code", "year")
) %>%
drop_na(fossil_share_elec, co2_per_capita, schooling, gdp_per_capita)
head(merged_yr)
write_csv(
merged_yr,
here("output", paste0("merged_dataset_", target_year, "_complete_cases.csv"))
)

5 Descriptive statistics

df_desc <- merged_yr %>%
select(fossil_share_elec, co2_per_capita, schooling, gdp_per_capita) %>%
na.omit()

cat("Number of observations:", nrow(df_desc), "\n\n")
## Number of observations: 160
summary(df_desc)
##  fossil_share_elec co2_per_capita     schooling      gdp_per_capita    
##  Min.   :  0.00    Min.   : 0.056   Min.   : 1.412   Min.   :   253.4  
##  1st Qu.: 25.50    1st Qu.: 1.060   1st Qu.: 7.359   1st Qu.:  2490.6  
##  Median : 59.44    Median : 3.381   Median :10.131   Median :  6495.5  
##  Mean   : 55.10    Mean   : 4.710   Mean   : 9.412   Mean   : 15441.0  
##  3rd Qu.: 84.71    3rd Qu.: 5.753   3rd Qu.:11.845   3rd Qu.: 18859.5  
##  Max.   :100.00    Max.   :40.128   Max.   :14.296   Max.   :104590.1

5.1 Boxplots

op <- par(no.readonly = TRUE)
par(mfrow = c(2, 2), mar = c(4, 4, 5, 2), oma = c(0, 0, 2, 0))

boxplot(
df_desc$fossil_share_elec,
main = expression(bold(atop("Fossil Fuel Share of Electricity", "(%) Across Countries in 2023"))),
ylab = expression(bold("Fossil Fuel Share (%)")),
col = "lightblue"
)

boxplot(
df_desc$co2_per_capita,
main = expression(bold(atop(CO[2]~"Emissions per Capita", "(Tonnes per Capita) in 2023"))),
ylab = expression(bold(CO[2]~"Emissions (Tonnes)")),
col = "lightblue"
)

boxplot(
df_desc$schooling,
main = expression(bold(atop("Mean Years of Schooling", "(Years) Across Countries in 2023"))),
ylab = expression(bold("Schooling (Years)")),
col = "lightblue"
)

boxplot(
df_desc$gdp_per_capita,
main = expression(bold(atop("GDP per Capita", "(US$ per Capita) in 2023"))),
ylab = expression(bold("GDP per Capita (US$)")),
col = "lightblue"
)

mtext("Descriptive Statistics: Distribution of Variables", outer = TRUE, cex = 1.2, font = 2)

par(op)

5.2 Correlation matrix

cor_matrix <- round(
cor(df_desc, use = "pairwise.complete.obs"),
3
)

kable(cor_matrix, caption = "Correlation Matrix (2023)")
Correlation Matrix (2023)
fossil_share_elec co2_per_capita schooling gdp_per_capita
fossil_share_elec 1.000 0.294 -0.100 -0.176
co2_per_capita 0.294 1.000 0.422 0.482
schooling -0.100 0.422 1.000 0.587
gdp_per_capita -0.176 0.482 0.587 1.000

5.3 Scatterplot matrix

panel.lm <- function(x, y, ...) {
points(x, y, pch = 16, col = rgb(0.27, 0.51, 0.71, alpha = 0.6))
abline(lm(y ~ x), col = "red", lty = 2, lwd = 1.5)
}

pairs(
df_desc,
upper.panel = panel.lm,
lower.panel = panel.lm,
labels = c(
"Fossil Share\n(%)",
expression(CO[2]~"\n(Tonnes)"),
"Schooling\n(Years)",
"GDP per Cap\n(US$)"
),
main = "Scatterplot Matrix with Linear Trend Lines"
)

6 Modelling

6.1 Winsor function and model dataframe

winsor <- function(x, probs = c(0.01, 0.99)) {
qs <- quantile(x, probs = probs, na.rm = TRUE, type = 7)
pmin(pmax(x, qs[1]), qs[2])
}

df_model <- merged_yr %>%
transmute(
fossil_share = as.numeric(fossil_share_elec),
co2_pc       = as.numeric(co2_per_capita),
schooling    = as.numeric(schooling),
gdp_pc       = as.numeric(gdp_per_capita)
) %>%
filter(complete.cases(fossil_share, co2_pc, schooling, gdp_pc)) %>%
mutate(
co2_pc_w = winsor(co2_pc, probs = c(0.01, 0.99)),
co2_shift = ifelse(min(co2_pc_w, na.rm = TRUE) <= 0,
abs(min(co2_pc_w, na.rm = TRUE)) + 1e-6, 0),
ln_co2_pc = log(co2_pc_w + co2_shift),
ln_gdp = log(gdp_pc),
fossil_share_c = fossil_share - mean(fossil_share, na.rm = TRUE),
schooling_c    = schooling    - mean(schooling, na.rm = TRUE)
)

6.2 Three models

m1 <- lm(ln_co2_pc ~ fossil_share_c + ln_gdp, data = df_model)
m2 <- lm(ln_co2_pc ~ fossil_share_c + ln_gdp + schooling_c, data = df_model)
m3 <- lm(ln_co2_pc ~ fossil_share_c + ln_gdp + schooling_c + fossil_share_c:schooling_c,data = df_model)

6.3 Robust standard errors (HC1)

se_m1_robust <- sqrt(diag(vcovHC(m1, type = "HC1")))[names(coef(m1))]
se_m2_robust <- sqrt(diag(vcovHC(m2, type = "HC1")))[names(coef(m2))]
se_m3_robust <- sqrt(diag(vcovHC(m3, type = "HC1")))[names(coef(m3))]

6.4 Regression table

stargazer(
  m1, m2, m3,
  type = "html",
  title = "<span style='color: black; font-weight: bold;'> Table 2. Regression results of three models",
  dep.var.labels = "log(CO2 emissions per capita)",
  se = list(se_m1_robust, se_m2_robust, se_m3_robust),
  digits = 3,
  model.numbers = TRUE,
  order = c("fossil_share_c", "ln_gdp", "schooling_c", "fossil_share_c:schooling_c"),
  covariate.labels = c(
    "Fossil fuel share of electricity (centered)",
    "log(GDP per capita)",
    "Average years of schooling (centered)",
    "Fossil share × Schooling"
  ),
  notes.append = FALSE,
  notes = "Significance levels: * p < 0.05; ** p < 0.01; *** p < 0.001. <br> Robust standard errors (HC1) in parentheses.",
  header = FALSE
)
Table 2. Regression results of three models
Dependent variable:
log(CO2 emissions per capita)
(1) (2) (3)
Fossil fuel share of electricity (centered) 0.010*** 0.010*** 0.011***
(0.002) (0.002) (0.002)
log(GDP per capita) 0.001**
(0.0005)
Average years of schooling (centered) 0.839*** 0.615*** 0.633***
(0.041) (0.067) (0.071)
Fossil share × Schooling 0.120*** 0.118***
(0.028) (0.028)
Constant -6.558*** -4.576*** -4.723***
(0.373) (0.598) (0.627)
Observations 160 160 160
R2 0.775 0.802 0.811
Adjusted R2 0.772 0.799 0.806
Residual Std. Error 0.633 (df = 157) 0.595 (df = 156) 0.584 (df = 155)
F Statistic 270.607*** (df = 2; 157) 211.239*** (df = 3; 156) 166.210*** (df = 4; 155)
Note: Significance levels: * p < 0.05; ** p < 0.01; *** p < 0.001.
Robust standard errors (HC1) in parentheses.

6.5 Simple slope analysis

s_slopes <- sim_slopes(
  model = m3,
  pred  = "fossil_share_c",
  modx  = "schooling_c",
  johnson_neyman = TRUE,
  cond.int = TRUE,
  robust = TRUE
)
print(s_slopes)
## JOHNSON-NEYMAN INTERVAL
## 
## When schooling_c is OUTSIDE the interval [-51.10, -4.35], the slope of
## fossil_share_c is p < .05.
## 
## Note: The range of observed values of schooling_c is [-8.00, 4.88]
## 
## SIMPLE SLOPES ANALYSIS
## 
## When schooling_c = -3.155820e+00 (- 1 SD): 
## 
##                                 Est.   S.E.   t val.      p
## ----------------------------- ------ ------ -------- ------
## Slope of fossil_share_c         0.01   0.00     3.04   0.00
## Conditional intercept           0.50   0.11     4.70   0.00
## 
## When schooling_c =  7.438494e-16 (Mean): 
## 
##                                 Est.   S.E.   t val.      p
## ----------------------------- ------ ------ -------- ------
## Slope of fossil_share_c         0.01   0.00     6.78   0.00
## Conditional intercept           0.87   0.05    17.98   0.00
## 
## When schooling_c =  3.155820e+00 (+ 1 SD): 
## 
##                                 Est.   S.E.   t val.      p
## ----------------------------- ------ ------ -------- ------
## Slope of fossil_share_c         0.01   0.00     6.41   0.00
## Conditional intercept           1.24   0.10    11.96   0.00
cat("### Table 3a: Simple Slopes (4-digit precision)\n")
## ### Table 3a: Simple Slopes (4-digit precision)
s_slopes$slopes %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  kable(digits = 4, caption = "Slopes of fossil_share_c at different schooling levels")
Slopes of fossil_share_c at different schooling levels
Value of schooling_c Est. S.E. 2.5% 97.5% t val. p
-3.1558 0.0067 0.0022 0.0023 0.0111 3.0388 0.0028
0.0000 0.0105 0.0016 0.0074 0.0136 6.7754 0.0000
3.1558 0.0143 0.0022 0.0099 0.0187 6.4055 0.0000

6.6 Interaction Plot

interact_plot(
  model = m3,
  pred  = "fossil_share_c",
  modx  = "schooling_c",
  interval = TRUE,
  plot.points = TRUE,
  point.alpha = 0.3,
  x.label = "Fossil fuel share of electricity (centered)",
  y.label = "log(CO2 emissions per capita)",
  main.title = "Interaction: Schooling x Fossil Share (log CO2)"
)

7 Model diagnostics

par(mfrow = c(2, 2), mar = c(4.5, 4.5, 3, 2), oma = c(0, 0, 2, 0))
plot(
m3,
pch = 20,
col = rgb(120/255, 170/255, 215/255, alpha = 0.7),
lwd = 1.5,
sub.caption = ""
)
mtext("Regression Diagnostics for Moderation Model (log CO2)", outer = TRUE, cex = 1.2, font = 2)

par(mfrow = c(1, 1))

8 VIF and model comparison

vif_m3 <- vif(m3)
vif_m2 <- vif(m2)
vif_comparison <- data.frame(
Variable = names(vif_m3),
VIF_with_Interaction = as.numeric(vif_m3),
VIF_without_Interaction = as.numeric(vif_m2[names(vif_m3)])
)

kable(vif_comparison, digits = 3, caption = "VIF Comparison (with vs without interaction)")
VIF Comparison (with vs without interaction)
Variable VIF_with_Interaction VIF_without_Interaction
fossil_share_c 1.014 1.012
ln_gdp 2.981 2.941
schooling_c 2.963 2.961
fossil_share_c:schooling_c 1.030 NA

9 F-test (Model 2 vs Model 3)

f_test_m2_m3 <- anova(m2, m3)
kable(f_test_m2_m3, caption = "F-test: Compare Model 2 vs Model 3")
F-test: Compare Model 2 vs Model 3
Res.Df RSS Df Sum of Sq F Pr(>F)
156 55.25637 NA NA NA NA
155 52.88496 1 2.371419 6.95037 0.0092328
